errors: [1. The value set "Reason for not performing Chlamydia Screening" is marked as TBD, which may prevent accurate documentation of clinical rationale. 2. The logic for "No Screening" includes a check for ProcedureRequest.orderedOn SAME_OR_AFTER Today, which may incorrectly exclude patients with future-scheduled screenings that have not yet been executed. This could lead to missed opportunities for screening if the scheduled procedure is not completed.]

[Age threshold starts at 40 years, which may conflict with guidelines recommending 45-50 as starting age (e.g., USPSTF). Time interval uses 1-year lookback for mammogram, but major guidelines (e.g., USPSTF) recommend 2-year intervals for screening.]

[Time Intervals (Lookback Periods) - The lookback period for Colonoscopy is specified as 1 month in the ELM implementation, but clinical guidelines typically recommend a 10-year interval for average-risk colon cancer screening. The 1-month lookback would incorrectly exclude patients who had a colonoscopy more than a month ago but within the recommended 10-year window.]

[Time Interval Mismatch: The ELM specifies a 1-month lookback period for procedures in the Assessment logic, but standard prostate cancer screening guidelines typically recommend a longer interval (e.g., annual screening). A 1-month lookback may lead to overly frequent screening recommendations, which is not clinically reasonable.]

The time interval for "Has Recent DEXA Scan" is set to 2 years, but clinical guidelines (e.g., USPSTF) recommend screening every 10-15 years for average-risk women with normal initial results. A 2-year lookback period is too short and would inappropriately exclude patients who had a scan more than 2 years ago but are still within the recommended interval. This discrepancy could lead to unnecessary repeat testing.

[HbA1c threshold of 9 g% exceeds standard clinical criteria for diabetes diagnosis (typically 6.5% or higher). This creates an overly restrictive diagnostic criterion that would fail to identify patients with Type 2 Diabetes.]

[Age thresholds (60-75 years) may not align with standard clinical guidelines which typically recommend starting screening at 45-50 years and continuing until 75. The 2-year lookback period for recent colorectal screening is also inconsistent with standard intervals (e.g., 10 years for colonoscopy), potentially leading to over-screening.]

[BMI threshold for obesity is set to 35 kg/m2 in ELM, but standard clinical definitions classify obesity as BMI ≥30 kg/m2. This discrepancy may exclude eligible patients who meet the standard obesity criteria but have BMI between 30-34.9 kg/m2.]

[Age thresholds in ELM (30-65 years) do not match CPG recommendation (21-65 years) for cervical cancer screening. The In Demographic logic excludes women aged 21-29 who are included in the USPSTF guidelines.]

[Age threshold in ELM is set to 75 years, but the CPG rationale references USPSTF recommendations for adults 65 years or older. This discrepancy excludes patients aged 65-74 who should be included per the clinical guideline.]
[Age threshold in ELM is set to >=25 years, but the CPG (USPSTF) recommends screening for adults 18 years or older. This discrepancy excludes adults aged 18-24 from the target population.]

The "Has Recent DEXA Scan" logic uses a 6-month lookback period, but clinical guidelines (e.g., USPSTF) typically recommend a 2-year interval for determining recent screening. This mismatch may lead to over-screening by excluding patients who had a DEXA scan within the past 6 months, whereas the CPG intends to avoid redundant testing within a 2-year window.

[Age thresholds in ELM (55-75 years) do not match CPG guidance (65-75 years). The In Demographic condition includes ages 55-75, whereas the clinical recommendation specifies men aged 65-75 years who have ever smoked.]

[MeetsInclusionCriteria uses logical OR between Screening Age and Gender, but clinical guidelines require both conditions (age >=40 AND female gender) to define the target population. The correct inclusion criteria should be Screening Age AND Gender, not OR.]
[Age threshold missing upper limit (USPSTF recommends 50-80 years), Smoking history criteria missing pack-year requirement (USPSTF requires 20 pack-year history)]

[Needs Falls Prevention Screening logic is inverted. The ELM currently requires both In Demographic AND Has Recent Falls Assessment, but the CPG likely intends to target patients who are in the demographic AND have NOT had a recent falls assessment (within the last year).
